message:
test(robot_envelope): metamorphic oracles for canonicalize_json

The canonicalizer is the gatekeeper between runtime-variable bridge
output (wall-clock timestamps, HashMap iteration order, platform-specific
float noise) and the byte-exact golden JSON fixtures under
tests/golden_robot_envelope/. Golden tests alone pin a handful of concrete
payload shapes; metamorphic relations pin the invariants the
canonicalizer must uphold for *every* shape.

## Exposure change

`canonicalize_json` moves from a private helper inside the `tests` mod
to `pub fn canonicalize_json(value: &mut Value, parent_key: Option<&str>)`
on the module surface. The duplicated test-mod copy is removed; existing
golden tests pick up the public fn via `use super::*;`. No semantic
change, identical body.

## Five (actually six) relations

- MR1 idempotence — canon(canon(x)) == canon(x). Catches any future
  change that makes the transformation depend on application order,
  which would flake golden runs on re-canonicalized fixtures.
- MR2 timestamp erasure — two envelopes that differ ONLY in the value of
  a `timestamp` string field must canon equally. Pins the "[TIMESTAMP]"
  replacement as strictly value-ignoring.
- MR3 sort-invariance under magic keys — `agents`, `tags`, `pane_ids`
  arrays canon equally under any permutation. Pins the set-semantic
  assumption so a HashMap-based producer can't leak iteration-order
  into fixtures.
- MR4 order preservation under non-magic keys — arrays under any OTHER
  key must NOT be sorted; reversing them must produce a different canon.
  The dual of MR3: protects against a future refactor that "helpfully"
  sorts all arrays and silently breaks fixtures whose semantics depend
  on order.
- MR5 float precision clamp — two floats that round to the same 4dp
  value must canon equally. Absorbs libc / fma cross-platform noise.
- MR6 integer passthrough — i64 values survive canon byte-exactly. Pins
  the `fract() != 0.0` gate so integers outside f64's 2^53 lossless
  range aren't silently mangled through a float round-trip.

## Harness

tests/metamorphic_robot_envelope_canon.rs, proptest-driven with 200-300
cases per relation. Bounded-depth (3) + bounded-breadth (6) JSON
generator keeps shrinking cheap. All MRs compare canonical rendered
strings rather than raw Values so serde_json::Map insertion order is
deterministic.

## Verification

  cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test metamorphic_robot_envelope_canon

6 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored in 1.08s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
